mission.title: Install and Configure Monocle on Kubernetes
mission.description: Monocle is a framework designed for tracing GenAI app code, allowing developers to easily instrument their applications for observability. Installing Monocle enables you to monitor and trace your GenAI applications in production environments seamlessly.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for Monocle
steps.description: First, create a dedicated namespace for Monocle to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Apply Monocle Manifests
steps.description: Next, apply the Monocle manifests from the GitHub repository. Make sure to use a specific version instead of :latest.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check if the Monocle pods are running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Monocle will have the Monocle pods running in the 'monocle' namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and set up monitoring in your application code.

uninstall.title: Remove Monocle Resources
uninstall.description: To uninstall Monocle, delete the resources you applied earlier.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Monocle
upgrade.description: To upgrade Monocle, apply the new version of the manifests. Make sure to specify the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: If the Monocle pod is not starting, check the pod logs for errors using the command: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n monocle`.

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description: If the service is not accessible, ensure that the service type is set correctly (e.g., LoadBalancer or NodePort) and check the service configuration with: `kubectl get svc -n monocle`.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter resource issues, verify that your cluster has enough CPU and memory resources available for the Monocle pods.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
